perturbation.original_text: Section 2. Promoter's Fee. For providing services as set forth herein, VNUE will compensate Promoter i) Two Thousand Five Hundred Dollars ($2,500.00) for each One (1) Year contract Promoter secures per venue and Five Thousand Dollars ($5,000,00) for each Two (2) Year contract Promoter secures per venue, with payment due to Promoter within Thirty (30) Days from the date on which each such contract is countersigned; and ii) through the issuance of VNUE common stock as set forth below, and for the purposes of Rule 144 such shares of stock shall be deemed to have fully earned by Promoter upon the date of each issuance of such stock certificates by VStock Transfer: Three Million (3,000,000) shares of VNUE common stock shall be awarded to Promoter for performing Promotion Services as follows: Source: VNUE, INC., 8-K, 9/14/2015 For every Five (5) music venues that sign a contract with VNUE, Six Hundred Thousand (600,000) shares of VNUE common stock shall be awarded to Promoter; and If Ten (10) music venues sign a contract with VNUE before January 16, 2016, Promoter will receive an additional bonus of Three Hundred Thousand (300,000) shares of VNUE common stock.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 2. Promoter's Fee. For providing services as set forth herein, VNUE will compensate Promoter i) Two Thousand Five Hundred Dollars ($2,500.00) for each One (1) Year contract Promoter secures per venue and Five Thousand Dollars ($5,000,00) for each Two (2) Year contract Promoter secures per venue, with payment due to Promoter within Ninety (90) Days from the date on which each such contract is countersigned; and ii) through the issuance of VNUE common stock as set forth below, and for the purposes of Rule 144 such shares of stock shall be deemed to have fully earned by Promoter upon the date of each issuance of such stock certificates by VStock Transfer: Three Million (3,000,000) shares of VNUE common stock shall be awarded to Promoter for performing Promotion Services as follows: Source: VNUE, INC., 8-K, 9/14/2015 For every Five (5) music venues that sign a contract with VNUE, Six Hundred Thousand (600,000) shares of VNUE common stock shall be awarded to Promoter; and If Ten (10) music venues sign a contract with VNUE before January 16, 2016, Promoter will receive an additional bonus of Three Hundred Thousand (300,000) shares of VNUE common stock.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'Thirty (30) Days' to 'Ninety (90) Days' for payment of the cash fee contradicts Section 3, which states 'VNUE shall reimburse Promoter within Thirty (30) Days of receipt of such accounting', creating uncertainty about the actual payment terms for expenses.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 3. Expenses. VNUE shall reimburse Promoter for expenses incurred by Promoter while performing the duties herein. Promoter shall deliver to VNUE an itemized accounting of expenses incurred on a weekly basis, and VNUE shall reimburse Promoter within Thirty (30) Days of receipt of such accounting.
perturbation.justification: The change modifies the payment timeframe for the Promoter's fee from 'Thirty (30) Days' to 'Ninety (90) Days' in Section 2. This directly contradicts Section 3, which states 'VNUE shall reimburse Promoter within Thirty (30) Days of receipt of such accounting.' The contradiction lies in the conflicting payment terms, creating uncertainty as to which timeframe is applicable for reimbursements, thus fulfilling the definition of a Structural Flaw - In Text Contradiction. The change directly breaks compatibility with the contradicted section and creates inconsistency

perturbation.original_text: Section 6. Term. This Agreement shall commence on September 10, 2015 and shall continue for One (1) Year (the "Term"). At any time prior to the end of the Term, the Parties may agree in writing to extend the Agreement for successive One (1) Year periods (the "Renewal Terms") under the same conditions set forth herein.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 6. Term. This Agreement shall commence on September 10, 2015 and shall continue indefinitely (the "Term"). At any time prior to the end of what would have been the Term, the Parties may agree in writing to terminate the Agreement. This Agreement shall automatically renew for additional One (1) Year periods (the "Renewal Terms") unless terminated by either party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'One (1) Year' to 'indefinitely' and adding that the agreement 'shall automatically renew' after one year contradicts Section 6's original condition that renewal requires a written agreement by both parties. This creates confusion about the contract's duration and renewal process.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 6. Term. This Agreement shall commence on September 10, 2015 and shall continue indefinitely (the "Term"). At any time prior to the end of what would have been the Term, the Parties may agree in writing to terminate the Agreement. This Agreement shall automatically renew for additional One (1) Year periods (the "Renewal Terms") unless terminated by either party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a contradiction because it states the agreement continues 'indefinitely' and 'shall automatically renew' annually unless terminated, directly conflicting with the original condition in Section 6 that renewal requires a written agreement by both parties. The new language implies automatic renewal without explicit agreement, a clear departure from the original terms and thus a contradiction, and adheres to the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Section 12. Non-exclusion. It is understood that VNUE does not agree to use BookingEntertainment.com exclusively as its Promoter, and that Promoter shall not be held liable for the actions of third parties which may also be providing the same or similar services during the term of this Agreement. Likewise, Promoter is free to contract for services to be performed for other public and private companies while under contract with VNUE, subject to the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 12. Non-exclusion. It is understood that VNUE will exclusively use BookingEntertainment.com as its Promoter, and that Promoter shall not be held liable for the actions of third parties which may also be providing the same or similar services during the term of this Agreement. Likewise, Promoter is not free to contract for services to be performed for other public and private companies while under contract with VNUE.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'does not agree to use exclusively' to 'will exclusively use' directly contradicts the original non-exclusivity clause. Also, by adding 'Promoter is not free to contract', creates a situation where the promoter is not allowed to work with other companies, directly contracting the intent of the non-exclusion section, which originally allows the promoter work to for other companies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 12. Non-exclusion. It is understood that VNUE will exclusively use BookingEntertainment.com as its Promoter, and that Promoter shall not be held liable for the actions of third parties which may also be providing the same or similar services during the term of this Agreement. Likewise, Promoter is not free to contract for services to be performed for other public and private companies while under contract with VNUE.
perturbation.justification: The change directly inverts the non-exclusivity agreement, stating VNUE *will* exclusively use BookingEntertainment.com, which contradicts the original intention where VNUE *did not agree* to exclusive use. The further restriction on the Promoter working with other companies, going from 'free to contract' to 'not free to contract', creates a direct conflict with the concept of non-exclusivity. Both of these alterations in the changed text make it incompatible with the initial understanding and intent of the non-exclusion clause. The Contradicted text and changed text are identical in this case, however, the change in the original text to this new form clearly creates an in-text contradiction because it goes from Non-exclusive to Exclusive, directly conflicting with the purpose of the clause.
